title: Toradora!
desc: Two misunderstood high school students form an unlikely alliance to help each other win over their respective crushes, only to inevitably fall in love with one another, culminating in a dramatic runaway and a secret engagement.
wiki: Ryuji Takasu is a gentle guy cursed with an intimidating glare, while Taiga Aisaka is a tiny, fiery girl known as the 'Palmtop Tiger'. After discovering they have crushes on each other's best friends, Yusaku Kitamura and Minori Kushieda, they make a pact to help one another out. As they spend more time together plotting, sharing meals, and bickering, their alliance slowly blossoms into a genuine, heartfelt romance. Later in the story, during a school ski trip, a delirious Taiga accidentally confesses her true feelings for Ryuji to him. After their mutual friends force them to confront their reality, Ryuji and Taiga decide to elope to escape their complicated family situations, sharing a kiss and promising to marry each other. Although Taiga temporarily leaves to resolve her relationship with her estranged mother, the series concludes with a happy ending where she returns for their high school graduation and the two happily reunite.

title: Maid Sama!
desc: A strict student council president hides her secret part-time job at a maid café from her peers, until the school's most popular boy finds out, leading to a teasing dynamic that ultimately blossoms into a devoted relationship.
wiki: Misaki Ayuzawa is a fierce, boy-hating student council president who secretly works at a maid café to support her impoverished family. When Takumi Usui, the enigmatic and highly popular boy in school, discovers her secret, he chooses to keep it and instead becomes a regular at her café. What starts as him teasing her develops into a fiercely protective and highly competitive romance. Throughout the story, Usui constantly comes to Misaki's rescue, and it is eventually revealed that he has a complicated past as the illegitimate son of a wealthy British noble family. The series builds to a climax where they finally admit their mutual feelings and officially become a couple, concluding with a happy ending where they remain together and eventually marry years later.

title: Kimi ni Todoke: From Me to You
desc: A misunderstood girl who looks like a horror movie character falls for the most popular boy in school, eventually overcoming her social anxiety to confess her feelings and start a sweet, official relationship with him.
wiki: Sawako Kuronuma is feared by her classmates due to her resemblance to Sadako from 'The Ring,' leading to constant misunderstandings. Her life completely changes when the outgoing Shota Kazehaya befriends her. As she makes her first real friends, Ayane and Chizuru, Sawako's confidence grows. Her relationship with Kazehaya deepens over time, though they face hurdles like school rumors and a romantic rival, Ume Kurumizawa. During the second-year school festival, Sawako finally gathers the courage to confess her love to Kazehaya through a closed door, and he warmly reciprocates. The series builds to a happy ending where they officially start dating, supporting each other through their high school graduation and toward their future career paths.
img: /static/Kimi ni Todoke: From Me to You.jpeg

title: Golden Time
desc: A college student with amnesia falls in love with an eccentric girl, but their relationship is severely tested as his past memories slowly return, culminating in him accepting both versions of himself to secure a happy future with her.
wiki: Banri Tada loses his memories after falling off a bridge shortly after his high school graduation. Starting fresh at a Tokyo law school, he meets and falls in love with the dramatic and fiercely loyal Kouko Kaga. However, their relationship becomes highly complicated by the lingering spirit of Banri's past self, who still loves his childhood friend, Linda. As Banri's old memories begin to overwrite his new ones, he struggles with the terrifying prospect of losing Kouko and the new life he has built. After a period of intense emotional turmoil and a temporary breakup for Kouko's sake, Banri manages to reconcile his past and present identities. In a heartwarming happy ending, Banri retains his memories of Kouko, and the two happily reunite on the bridge where his accident occurred, exchanging promise rings.

title: Lovely Complex
desc: A tall girl and a short boy struggle with their feelings and societal expectations regarding their height difference, eventually overcoming their insecurities to become an official, loving couple.
wiki: Risa Koizumi (who is taller than the average Japanese girl) and Atsushi Otani (who is shorter than the average boy) are known as a comedy duo by their classmates due to their constant bickering and height disparity. They initially team up to help each other secure their respective crushes but fail. As they spend more time together, Risa realizes she has genuine romantic feelings for Otani. Otani initially rejects her confession, struggling to see her as more than a friend and navigating his own deep-seated insecurities about their heights. However, Risa's persistent and genuine affection eventually wears him down. After a series of comedic and dramatic events, Otani realizes his own feelings and kisses her, finally making them a couple. The story concludes with a highly satisfying happy ending, showing them graduating high school while remaining happily together and entirely confident in their relationship.

title: Horimiya
desc: A popular girl and a gloomy boy discover each other's hidden true selves, sparking a fast-paced and genuine romance that culminates in a heartfelt marriage proposal.
wiki: At school, Kyoko Hori is a popular and smart student, while Izumi Miyamura comes across as a gloomy, distant nerd. In reality, Hori is a busy homebody who takes care of her little brother, and Miyamura is a gentle guy covered in piercings and tattoos. When they accidentally meet outside of school, they agree to keep each other's secrets. This shared secret quickly blossoms into a romantic relationship. Unlike many romances, they start dating early on and are physically and emotionally intimate. As Miyamura integrates into Hori's friend group, he cuts his hair and overcomes his middle school trauma. The series reaches a definitive happy ending when Miyamura proposes to Hori before graduation; she happily accepts, and they graduate together, fully embracing the future as a couple.

title: Wotakoi: Love is Hard for Otaku
desc: Childhood friends reunite as office workers and start dating, navigating the comedic challenges of balancing their adult responsibilities with their hardcore otaku lifestyles to build a supportive, lasting relationship.
wiki: Narumi Momose, a secret fujoshi, reunites with her childhood friend Hirotaka Nifuji, a hardcore gamer, at her new office job. Tired of having to hide her hobbies from non-otaku boyfriends, Narumi accepts Hirotaka's practical proposal to date him since they can be their true selves around each other. The series explores the romance between working adults, focusing on the struggles of balancing adult responsibilities with their nerdy passions. Alongside their friends and coworkers, Hanako Koyanagi and Taro Kabakura (who eventually get married), Narumi and Hirotaka slowly bridge the gap between childhood friends and romantic partners. The story provides a continuous happy ending, showing them growing more affectionately devoted to one another while remaining proudly geeky.

title: My Love Story!!
desc: A giant, intimidating boy with a heart of gold falls in love with a tiny, sweet girl, leading to an incredibly wholesome and drama-free relationship where they continually prove their devotion to one another.
wiki: Takeo Gouda is a massive, intimidating high schooler who is wildly popular with the boys but scares away girls, who typically fall for his handsome best friend, Makoto Sunakawa. When Takeo saves a sweet girl named Rinko Yamato from a groper on a train, he instantly falls for her but assumes she likes Sunakawa. In a heartwarming twist, Sunakawa helps clear up the misunderstanding, revealing that Yamato is entirely infatuated with Takeo. They become a couple very early in the story. Rather than dragging out a 'will-they-won't-they' dynamic, the series focuses on the pure, unadulterated joy of their relationship. They face minor insecurities and external challenges together, culminating in a delightfully wholesome happy ending where their unwavering love and mutual respect are stronger than ever.

title: Tonikawa: Over The Moon For You
desc: A brilliant boy marries a mysterious girl who saved his life, diving headfirst into a sweet and domestic newlywed life filled with endless joyful moments and unconditional love.
wiki: Nasa Yuzaki, an academic genius, is saved from a fatal traffic accident by a beautiful, mysterious girl named Tsukasa. Bleeding and desperate, he confesses his love to her on the spot. She agrees to be with him on one absolute condition: they must get married. Years later, on his 18th birthday, she suddenly appears at his door with a marriage registration form. They legally marry that night and begin their life as newlyweds. While there are heavy hints and eventual revelations tying Tsukasa's immortality to the ancient tale of Princess Kaguya, the story primarily focuses on the incredibly fluffy, romantic, and comedic day-to-day life of a young married couple. It guarantees a happy ending as they navigate domestic bliss, constantly reaffirming their deep love and commitment to each other.

title: Kaguya-sama: Love Is War
desc: Two brilliant student council leaders turn their mutual crushes into a hilarious psychological battlefield, but eventually drop their pride to confess and build a genuinely supportive, loving relationship.
wiki: Miyuki Shirogane and Kaguya Shinomiya are the top student council representatives at the prestigious Shuchiin Academy. While they both have developed strong feelings for one another, their elite pride and stubbornness prevent either of them from confessing first, leading to high-stakes mental warfare. However, as the series progresses, their emotional walls break down. The climax of their 'war' occurs during the cultural festival, where Miyuki orchestrates a massive romantic spectacle, leading to their first kiss. They officially start dating shortly after. The story ultimately sees them overcoming the massive societal and family hurdles posed by Kaguya's wealthy, oppressive family, concluding with a happy ending where they head off to study at Stanford University together.

title: Snow White with the Red Hair
desc: A determined herbalist girl flees her home and meets a prince, leading to a beautifully mature romance where they mutually support each other's royal and professional ambitions.
wiki: Shirayuki is a cheerful herbalist with rare, apple-red hair. Fleeing a foolish prince who wants her as a concubine, she crosses borders and meets Zen Wistalia, the second prince of Clarines. Rather than being a damsel in distress, Shirayuki works hard to become an official court herbalist to stand by Zen's side on her own merit. Their relationship is built on deep mutual respect and healthy communication. Despite the political pushback regarding their difference in social status, Zen officially declares his intention to make Shirayuki his wife. The story guarantees a happy ending, focusing on their unwavering loyalty to each other as they overcome obstacles and build a future together.

title: Kamisama Kiss
desc: A homeless girl accidentally becomes a local Land God and falls in love with her grumpy fox familiar, embarking on a magical journey that transcends time to save him and secure their future.
wiki: Nanami Momozono is evicted and accidentally becomes a shrine deity after saving a mysterious man. She is forced to rely on Tomoe, a cynical and powerful fox yokai who serves as the shrine's familiar. While they initially clash, Nanami's kindness melts Tomoe's icy exterior, and they fall deeply in love. In the story's climax, Nanami travels back in time to cure a fatal curse placed on Tomoe, revealing that she was actually the woman he fell in love with centuries ago, effectively closing the time loop. The series concludes with the ultimate happy ending: Tomoe becomes human so he can grow old with Nanami, they get married, and are later shown happily raising their child together.

title: Spice and Wolf
desc: A traveling merchant and a wise wolf deity form a partnership that slowly blossoms into a profound romance, ultimately choosing a quiet, loving life together over endless wandering.
wiki: Kraft Lawrence, a traveling merchant, discovers the ancient wolf deity Holo hiding in his cart. They strike a deal to travel north to her homeland of Yoitsu. Along the way, they navigate complex economic schemes, trade disputes, and dangerous situations, constantly relying on Lawrence's business acumen and Holo's centuries of wisdom. Their constant banter evolves into a deep, inseparable bond. When forced to choose between his lifelong dream of owning a shop in a major city and his love for Holo, Lawrence chooses her. In a highly satisfying happy ending, they give up their nomadic lifestyle to settle down in a quiet hot spring town, get married, open a bathhouse called 'Spice and Wolf,' and have a daughter.

title: Banished from the Hero's Party, I Decided to Live a Quiet Life in the Countryside
desc: A former adventurer opens an apothecary in a frontier town and reunites with an old ally, quickly sparking a deeply affectionate and domestic romance that proves peaceful life is the ultimate reward.
wiki: After being deemed a burden and kicked out of the Hero's party, Red decides to leave the frontline and open a peaceful apothecary in the countryside town of Zoltan. His dream of a quiet life takes a wonderful turn when Rit, a beautiful princess and former adventuring ally, suddenly arrives, practically moves in, and helps him run the shop. Unlike slow-burn romances, Red and Rit become an official, fiercely loving couple very early on, sharing a highly affectionate and physical domestic life. They manage to protect their peaceful town from the lingering chaos of the Hero's quest, successfully saving Red's sister from her tragic destiny. The story locks in a happy ending with Red and Rit getting officially engaged and continuing their cozy, idyllic life together.

title: The Saint's Magic Power is Omnipotent
desc: A summoned office worker becomes a magical researcher and falls for a knight, eventually embracing her powers to save him and solidifying their romantic bond.
wiki: Sei is summoned to another world as a Saint but is initially ignored. She starts making abnormally effective potions and catches the eye of Knight Commander Albert Hawke. As their relationship deepens, Sei realizes her magical power is directly tied to her feelings of love for Albert. The story reaches a joyful climax when Sei fully accepts her role and her love, using her omnipotent saintly magic to eradicate a major monster threat and save Albert, leading to a happy continuation of their official romantic relationship.

title: I'm the Villainess, So I'm Taming the Final Boss
desc: To avoid a tragic fate, a reincarnated villainess successfully romances the Demon King, averting disaster and securing a triumphant, happy marriage.
wiki: Aileen suddenly remembers she has been reincarnated as the villainess in an otome game. To prevent her destined gruesome death, she boldly proposes to the game's final boss, Demon King Claude. Through her genuine efforts to help him and his demonic subjects, Claude falls deeply in love with her. Aileen successfully thwarts the game's original heroine (who turns out to be malicious), saves Claude from turning into a mindless monster, and secures a definitive happy ending by marrying him and becoming the Demon Empress.
img: /static/I'm the Villainess, So I'm Taming the Final Boss

title: 7th Time Loop
desc: A noblewoman marries the prince who killed her in previous lives, using her accumulated skills to prevent a devastating war and build a loving partnership.
wiki: Rishe has lived six different lives, each ending in a tragic death at age 20 due to a world war initiated by Emperor Arnold Hein. In her seventh loop, she unexpectedly receives a marriage proposal from a younger Arnold. Accepting the proposal to keep a close eye on him, Rishe uses the vast array of skills she learned in her past lives to subtly alter the political landscape and prevent the impending tragedy. Along the way, Arnold becomes genuinely captivated by her brilliance, and they develop a profound mutual respect and romantic bond, steering their future toward a peaceful and happy resolution.

title: Why Raeliana Ended Up at the Duke's Mansion
desc: A reincarnated girl makes a survival pact with a cunning duke, leading to a genuine romance where they overcome deadly political plots to marry and live happily.
wiki: Rinkoha reincarnates into a novel as Raeliana McMillan, a minor character destined to be poisoned by her fiancé. To survive, she blackmails the powerful, two-faced Duke Noah Wynknight into a fake engagement. While they initially use each other to fend off political enemies, they slowly fall in love. Raeliana discovers that the novel's original protagonist, Beatrice, is the true villain trying to steal her fate. With Noah's unwavering support, Raeliana defeats Beatrice. The story concludes with a highly satisfying happy ending where Noah formally proposes, and they marry, finally free from the novel's predetermined tragic plot.
img: /static/Why Raeliana Ended Up at the Duke's Mansion

title: My Happy Marriage
desc: An abused, seemingly powerless daughter is married off to a fearsome commander, only to find a deeply loving partner who helps her unlock her true, formidable abilities.
wiki: Miyo Saimori is treated like a servant by her abusive family for lacking supernatural abilities. She is married off to Kiyoka Kudou, a military commander rumored to be incredibly cruel. However, Kiyoka turns out to be kind and fiercely protective. He quickly realizes the abuse she suffered and begins to help her heal. It is eventually revealed that Miyo actually possesses the incredibly rare and powerful Dream Sight ability, which her mother sealed away to protect her. Together, they confront and defeat Miyo's abusive family and other supernatural threats, officially becoming engaged and cementing a deeply heartwarming, happy ending.

title: Tsuki ga Kirei
desc: Two middle schoolers navigate the awkwardness of first love, maintaining a long-distance relationship through high school that culminates in a happy marriage and a child.
wiki: Middle schoolers Kotaro and Akane end up in the same class and slowly grow closer through LINE messages, navigating the intense awkwardness and beauty of innocent young romance. When Akane is forced to move away for high school, they decide to try a long-distance relationship. The series is celebrated for its incredibly realistic depiction of adolescent love, avoiding overly dramatic tropes. In a definitive and deeply rewarding happy ending, the post-credits sequence reveals through a series of LINE messages and photos that they successfully stayed together through high school and college, eventually getting married and having a child together.

title: The Angel Next Door Spoils Me Rotten
desc: A neighborly act of kindness leads to a sweet domestic romance where two lonely teenagers heal each other and officially become a deeply affectionate couple.
wiki: After Amane lends his umbrella to Mahiru, the most beautiful and popular girl in school, she repays him by cleaning his messy apartment and cooking for him. What starts as a simple favor becomes a daily routine. Amane discovers that despite her 'angelic' persona at school, Mahiru is deeply lonely and estranged from her parents. By offering her a genuine, safe space and unconditional care, Amane melts her emotional barriers. The incredibly sweet and wholesome story progresses past their mutual denial, ending with a highly satisfying conclusion where they openly confess their feelings and officially start dating, no longer hiding their relationship from their peers.
img: /static/The Angel Next Door Spoils Me Rotten

title: A Sign of Affection
desc: A deaf college student falls for a multilingual upperclassman who eagerly learns her world, resulting in an incredibly healthy and communicative official relationship.
wiki: Yuki is a deaf college student whose sheltered world dramatically expands when she meets Itsuomi, a multilingual upperclassman with a passion for traveling. Instead of pitying her, Itsuomi is fascinated by her and immediately begins learning Japanese Sign Language so they can communicate directly. The series is lauded for its exceptionally healthy, drama-free approach to romance. They confess their feelings and officially become a couple quite early on. The story continues to follow their blossoming relationship as they meet each other's families, plan for a shared future, and maintain a joyful, supportive partnership with a guaranteed happy trajectory.

title: The Dangers in My Heart
desc: A boy who pretends to be an edgy loner falls for a quirky, popular girl, overcoming his insecurities to confess his feelings and start a wonderfully wholesome relationship.
wiki: Kyotaro Ichikawa reads murder encyclopedias and pretends to be a dark sociopath, fantasizing about killing the school's most popular girl, Anna Yamada. However, he quickly realizes she is actually a clumsy, food-obsessed airhead, and he begins secretly protecting her and helping her out. His 'murderous' urges are revealed to just be a massive, awkward crush. Over time, Yamada also falls deeply in love with Ichikawa's quiet kindness. They both grow immensely, with Ichikawa shedding his edgy persona and gaining self-confidence. The story reaches a highly satisfying milestone when they mutually confess their love and officially become an incredibly supportive, happy couple.

title: Shikimori's Not Just a Cutie
desc: A chronically unlucky boy enjoys a loving, established relationship with his cool, protective girlfriend, showcasing endless sweet moments and a happy, drama-free dynamic.
wiki: Izumi is a kind-hearted boy cursed with incredibly bad luck, constantly getting into minor accidents. However, his life is wonderful because he is already dating Shikimori. While she appears to be a standard cute, sweet girlfriend, she instantly transforms into a cool, sharp-eyed action hero whenever Izumi is in danger. Unlike many romances that focus on getting together, this series explores an already established couple. It completely avoids stressful drama and love triangles, opting instead to showcase their deep mutual devotion, constant communication, and a continuous stream of happily-ever-after moments throughout their high school lives.

title: Kubo Won't Let Me Be Invisible
desc: A practically invisible boy slowly comes out of his shell thanks to the playful affection of the only girl who notices him, eventually leading to a mutual confession.
wiki: Junta Shiraishi is a high schooler with such a severe lack of presence that people often assume his desk is empty. The only exception is his classmate Nagisa Kubo, who always spots him immediately. She playfully teases him, encouraging him to participate in class and making him experience a normal youth. As they spend time together, Kubo's teasing is revealed to be rooted in a deep crush, and Shiraishi slowly realizes his own romantic feelings for her while gaining the confidence to be seen by others. The story builds to a deeply heartwarming climax where Shiraishi finally confesses his love during a school trip, and Kubo happily accepts, cementing their happy ending.

title: Teasing Master Takagi-san
desc: A middle school boy constantly fails to outsmart the girl who teases him, a dynamic that ultimately blossoms into a happy marriage and a shared family life.
wiki: Middle schooler Nishikata is constantly teased by his desk neighbor, Takagi. He devises daily plans to get back at her and make her blush, but Takagi—who is secretly harboring a massive crush on him—always reads his mind and turns the tables, usually leaving him flustered. The series is a masterclass in innocent, slow-burn romantic comedy. The guarantee of their happy ending is actually baked into the franchise itself: the spin-off sequel series, 'Karakai Jouzu no (Moto) Takagi-san,' shows them as happily married adults living together with their adorable daughter, Chi, confirming that their wholesome childhood bond lasted a lifetime.

title: Fruits Basket (2019)
desc: A kind-hearted girl moves in with a family cursed to turn into zodiac animals, ultimately breaking their ancient curse and finding a lifelong romance.
wiki: Tohru Honda discovers the Soma family's curse and moves in with Yuki, Kyo, and Shigure. Her endless kindness helps heal the severe emotional trauma inflicted by the family head, Akito. As the story progresses, Tohru falls deeply in love with Kyo, the outcast Cat of the zodiac. Her unwavering love and acceptance become the catalyst that finally breaks the zodiac curse, freeing the family. The series concludes with a beautiful, definitive happy ending, showing Tohru and Kyo married, growing old together, and leaving behind a loving legacy.

title: Oregairu
desc: A cynical loner is forced to join a club that helps students, navigating complex social dynamics and a love triangle to finally find a genuine romantic connection.
wiki: Hachiman Hikigaya solves problems using self-sacrificial and cynical methods, putting him at odds with the other Service Club members, Yukino Yukinoshita and Yui Yuigahama. As the trio navigates complicated high school relationships, both girls fall in love with him. Hachiman's ultimate goal is to find something 'genuine'—a relationship without deceit or superficiality. He eventually chooses Yukino, and the two confess their profound feelings for one another, ending the series as an official, happily committed couple.

title: Rascal Does Not Dream of Bunny Girl Senpai
desc: A boy helps various girls suffering from supernatural phenomena, forming an unbreakable bond and a committed relationship with his upperclassman.
wiki: Sakuta Azusagawa meets teen actress Mai Sakurajima, who is suffering from 'Adolescence Syndrome' and becoming invisible to the world. After Sakuta goes to great lengths to save her, they quickly start dating. The series completely avoids the typical harem trap; Sakuta is fiercely loyal to Mai as he helps other girls with their supernatural issues. Despite facing heartbreaking timelines and reality-altering events in the sequel movie, Sakuta and Mai overcome tragedy, reunite, and secure their happy, loving relationship.
img: /static/Rascal Does Not Dream of Bunny Girl Senpai.jpeg

title: Kokoro Connect
desc: Five club members experience body-swapping and other supernatural phenomena that force them to confront their traumas, leading to a strong romantic conclusion.
wiki: A supernatural entity known as Heartseed forces the Cultural Research Club through strange trials like body-swapping and age regression. These phenomena strip away their emotional barriers and reveal their hidden traumas. Amidst the chaos, a love triangle develops between Taichi, Iori, and Inaba. While Taichi initially likes Iori, he eventually falls deeply in love with Inaba, who is fiercely protective of him. The series wraps up with Taichi confessing his feelings to Inaba, and they happily become an official couple.

title: Just Because!
desc: A transfer student reunites with his middle school friends in their final semester, sparking a realistic high school romance that ends in a mutual confession.
wiki: Eita Izumi returns to his hometown right before high school graduation, shaking up the stagnant relationships and lingering feelings of his old friends. The story focuses on a complicated web of unrequited loves, particularly the tension between Eita, his old crush Mio Natsume, and an underclassman named Ena Komiya. Both Eita and Mio work hard to pass the entrance exams for the same university. The story reaches a highly satisfying happy ending when they reunite at their new college, finally confess their mutual feelings for each other, and start dating.

title: Tada Never Falls in Love
desc: An aspiring photographer meets a foreign princess in Japan, overcoming the massive hurdle of her royal obligations to secure a surprisingly happy future together.
wiki: Mitsuyoshi Tada, a boy who has never known love, meets Teresa Wagner while taking photos. She joins his school's photography club, and they slowly fall in love. However, Teresa is actually the princess of the European country of Larsenburg and is forced to return home for an arranged marriage. Refusing to give up, Tada travels to Larsenburg to see her one last time. In a delightfully happy and triumphant ending, Teresa cancels her engagement, abdicates her restrictive royal duties, and returns to Japan to officially be with Tada.

title: 3D Kanojo: Real Girl
desc: An otaku boy starts dating the beautiful, misunderstood girl in school, overcoming medical tragedy to ultimately marry her years later.
wiki: Hikari Tsutsui is completely immersed in 2D media until the beautiful but ostracized Iroha Igarashi asks him out. Their relationship helps Hikari grow out of his shell. The story takes a dramatic turn when it is revealed Iroha has a brain tumor; the required surgery will cause her to lose her memories of Hikari. She is forced to move to America for the operation. Despite this tragedy, the series provides a definitive happy ending. Seven years later, they reunite. Iroha's memories eventually return, and the series ends with their beautiful wedding.

title: Itazura na Kiss
desc: A clumsy girl is forced to live with the genius boy who rejected her, slowly winning his heart over years of dedication that lead to marriage and a family.
wiki: Kotoko Aihara's house is destroyed by an earthquake, so her family moves in with Naoki Irie's family. Despite Naoki coldly rejecting her earlier, Kotoko's relentless love and unwavering support slowly chip away at his arrogant exterior. Uniquely for an anime, they actually get married halfway through the series. The second half follows their lives as young adults navigating college and their careers—Naoki becomes a doctor and Kotoko becomes a nurse to support him. The story locks in a wonderfully happy ending with the birth of their daughter, Kotomi.

title: The Quintessential Quintuplets
desc: A poor student becomes a tutor for five identical sisters, solving the mystery of his future bride in a heartwarming, definitive romantic conclusion.
wiki: Futaro Uesugi is hired to tutor the wealthy Nakano quintuplets, who all hate studying and are failing. Through his genuine dedication to their success, all five sisters—Ichika, Nino, Miku, Yotsuba, and Itsuki—fall in love with him. The series begins with a flash-forward to his wedding, creating a mystery about which sister he marries. Ultimately, Futaro realizes his deepest connection is with Yotsuba, who is revealed to be the girl he met in Kyoto during their childhood and who secretly supported him all along. He proposes to her, and the series concludes with their joyous, happy wedding.

title: Saekano: How to Raise a Boring Girlfriend
desc: An otaku recruits girls to help him create a dating sim game, ultimately realizing his 'boring' classmate was his true heroine all along, leading to a heartfelt confession and life together.
wiki: Tomoya Aki wants to make a visual novel using his incredibly ordinary classmate Megumi Kato as the main heroine, pulling together a team of talented but eccentric creators like Eriri and Utaha. Throughout the development of their game, the group faces creative differences and emotional hurdles. While the other girls harbor feelings for him, Tomoya eventually realizes that Megumi has been his greatest supporter and true love. In the concluding movie, Tomoya officially confesses to Megumi. The story ends with a beautiful flash-forward showing them as adults, running a successful company together and living in a happy, committed relationship.
img: /static/Saekano: How to Raise a Boring Girlfriend

title: Yamada-kun and the Seven Witches
desc: A delinquent discovers he can swap bodies by kissing, sparking a supernatural mystery that leads to a profound romance, a heartfelt proposal, and a happy marriage.
wiki: Ryu Yamada accidentally kisses top student Urara Shiraishi and discovers they can swap bodies. They revive the Supernatural Studies Club to uncover the identities of the seven school witches whose powers are activated by kissing. Through their trials, including dealing with memory manipulation and school politics, Yamada and Shiraishi fall deeply in love. Yamada eventually succeeds in erasing the witch powers from the school to protect his friends. The story guarantees a happy ending, concluding with Yamada and Shiraishi getting married and a flash-forward showing them happily raising their children.

title: Boarding School Juliet
desc: The leaders of two rival dormitories navigate a secret, forbidden romance, eventually changing their school's prejudiced society to openly celebrate their beautiful wedding.
wiki: Romio Inuzuka and Juliet Persia lead opposing factions, the Black Doggies and White Cats, at Dahlia Academy. Despite the bitter rivalry, Romio confesses his love to Juliet, and she accepts. They must keep their blossoming romance a total secret to avoid an all-out war between the students. However, their unwavering dedication to each other inspires them to change the academy's toxic culture. They eventually reveal their relationship to the world and successfully bridge the gap between their nations. The series finishes with a highly satisfying happy ending, culminating in Romio and Juliet's beautiful wedding ceremony surrounded by their friends.

title: Chivalry of a Failed Knight
desc: The worst student at a magic knight academy is challenged by a genius princess, forming a powerful partnership that culminates in an epic public proposal and engagement.
wiki: Ikki Kurogane is labeled a 'failed knight' due to his low magical power, but he possesses incredible physical skills. He is challenged to a duel by Stella Vermillion, a genius princess, and defeats her. They become roommates and partners, aiming for the top of the magical tournament. Unlike many action shows, they confess their feelings and officially start dating early on. The climax of the anime features Ikki overcoming severe physical and political trials to win his tournament match, immediately followed by him proposing to Stella in front of the entire stadium. She joyfully accepts, securing their happy future.

title: Sword Art Online
desc: Players trapped in a deadly virtual reality game find solace in each other, forging an unbreakable romantic bond that transcends the digital world into a devoted real-life relationship.
wiki: Kirito and Asuna are trapped in Aincrad, a VR MMORPG where dying in the game means dying in real life. Amidst the trauma and battles, they fall deeply in love, get married in-game, and adopt an AI daughter named Yui. Their romance becomes the core anchor for their survival. After Kirito clears the death game, he discovers Asuna is trapped in a new game, ALO, and fights to rescue her. The series repeatedly affirms their unshakeable loyalty to one another, firmly establishing them as a happy, devoted couple in the real world who continuously overcome adversity side-by-side.

title: The Familiar of Zero
desc: A terrible mage summons a human boy as her familiar, kicking off an epic adventure that transforms their constant bickering into a profound love and a cross-dimensional marriage.
wiki: Louise the Zero, mocked for her terrible magic skills, accidentally summons a normal human boy, Saito Hiraga, from Earth. Bound by a familiar's contract, they initially bicker constantly. However, Saito gains the ability to instantly master any weapon to protect her. Through surviving massive wars and political upheaval, their bond deepens into a fierce, protective love. The story concludes with the ultimate happy ending: Saito and Louise finally get legally married, and Saito uses a portal to bring his new wife back to modern-day Japan to introduce her to his parents.

title: Shakugan no Shana
desc: A boy discovers he is dead and joins forces with a fiery supernatural warrior, evolving from a simple torch into a powerful entity to secure a peaceful universe where they can be together.
wiki: Yuji Sakai discovers he is a 'Torch', a temporary remnant of a consumed human soul. He is saved by Shana, a fiery Flame Haze warrior. As they fight monsters together, Yuji refuses to fade away and they develop a deep romantic bond. In a massive plot twist in the final season, Yuji aligns with the enemy, becoming the Snake of the Festival, to create a new universe (Xanadu) where Flame Hazes and monsters no longer have to fight. After an epic final battle, Yuji and Shana reconcile, confess their eternal love for one another, and journey to the new world together, securing a definitive happy ending.

title: Record of Grancrest War
desc: A lone knight and a brilliant mage form a contract to unify a fractured continent, successfully achieving world peace and retiring to a happy, married life in the countryside.
wiki: Theo Cornaro, a wandering knight, and Siluca Meletes, a genius mage, form a magical contract with the ambitious goal of ending the chaotic wars across the land. As they build an army and outmaneuver rival lords, their professional partnership quickly evolves into a steadfast and deeply affectionate romance. They rely on each other implicitly. After achieving the impossible—unifying the continent and obtaining the Grancrest—Theo becomes the Emperor. However, he soon abdicates the throne to return to his peaceful homeland with Siluca, where the series ends with their beautiful wedding and a happily-ever-after.

title: Maoyu ~ Archenemy & Hero
desc: The Hero joins forces with the Demon King to solve the world's economic issues, forging an incredible partnership and a loving relationship that changes the world for the better.
wiki: Instead of fighting, the Hero and the Demon Queen sit down and realize that simply ending their war would cause economic collapse for both humans and demons. They forge a secret pact, teaming up to introduce new agricultural methods, economics, and education to advance society. The Hero fiercely protects the Demon Queen, and their mutual respect blossoms into a deep, unspoken love. They overcome political sabotage and religious persecution together. The story concludes on a hopeful and happy note, with the two of them walking away from the battlefield hand-in-hand to continue guiding the world from the shadows.

title: Special A
desc: A dense girl strives to beat her lifelong academic rival, eventually realizing he is desperately in love with her, leading to a globetrotting confession and a sweet relationship.
wiki: Hikari Hanazono has spent her life trying to defeat Kei Takishima, who always bests her and calls her 'Miss Rank Two'. She treats him as a fierce rival, completely oblivious to the fact that Kei is deeply, obviously, and passionately in love with her. Throughout the series, Kei quietly protects and supports her. When Kei is forced to transfer to a school in London due to family obligations, Hikari finally realizes her own romantic feelings. She flies across the world to confront him, leading to a highly satisfying happy ending where they finally share a mutual confession, a kiss, and officially become a couple.

title: Ouran High School Host Club
desc: A poor girl working as a host at an elite school slowly breaks through the dramatic antics of the club president, leading to a deeply genuine romance and an eventual happy marriage.
wiki: Haruhi Fujioka is mistaken for a boy and joins the Host Club to pay off her debt. The club president, Tamaki Suoh, slowly realizes he is genuinely in love with her, though his own familial trauma and density delay his confession. The story progresses beyond the episodic comedy into deep emotional territory as Haruhi helps Tamaki reconcile with his estranged family. In the definitive manga conclusion, they finally confess their mutual feelings, officially start dating, study abroad in Boston together, and eventually get happily married.

title: The World is Still Beautiful
desc: A rain-summoning princess marries a young, tyrannical sun king, transforming his cruel worldview into one of compassion and securing a deeply affectionate, lifelong marriage.
wiki: Nike Lemercier travels to the Sun Kingdom to marry King Livius. Despite his cruel reputation and their initial clashes, Nike's kindness and magical songs teach Livius how to appreciate the beauty of the world. Through surviving political assassination attempts and navigating the complexities of ruling, Livius grows into a benevolent king who is fiercely protective of Nike. The series cements a definitive happy ending, showing their unbreakable trust and deep, passionate love for one another as husband and wife.

title: Kakuriyo: Bed & Breakfast for Spirits
desc: A girl taken to the spirit realm refuses an arranged marriage, instead opening an eatery that slowly wins over the realm's inhabitants and ultimately leads her to genuinely fall for her ogre fiancé.
wiki: Aoi Tsubaki is spirited away to the Hidden Realm to marry the Odanna (an ogre god) to pay off her grandfather's massive debt. Refusing the marriage, she opens an eatery at his inn. Through her incredible cooking, she heals the hearts of the Ayakashi and resolves deep-seated realm conflicts. As the Odanna constantly protects and supports her, Aoi realizes he was the mysterious Ayakashi who saved her life when she was a starving child. She eventually falls deeply in love with him, willingly choosing to stay by his side for a happy future together.

title: Say 'I Love You'
desc: A quiet, cynical girl catches the interest of the most popular boy in school, slowly healing from past betrayals to build a devoted, trusting relationship that lasts a lifetime.
wiki: Mei Tachibana has isolated herself due to severe childhood bullying and betrayals. When Yamato Kurosawa takes an interest in her, he patiently helps her learn to trust and love others. They start dating early on, but the story focuses on them navigating realistic relationship hurdles like jealousy, malicious rumors, and communication issues. They overcome every obstacle, reinforcing their bond. The manga provides a highly satisfying conclusion where they stay together through adulthood, get married, and happily raise a child.

title: My Little Monster
desc: An emotionless top student and a violent but innocent delinquent balance each other's extremes, learning to navigate society and their own hearts to secure a happy marriage.
wiki: Shizuku Mizutani only cares about her grades until she is tasked with delivering handouts to Haru Yoshida, a suspended delinquent. Haru immediately attaches himself to her like a lost puppy. Their strange push-and-pull dynamic forces Shizuku to acknowledge her emotions and Haru to channel his chaotic energy into something productive. Over time, they realize they cannot live without each other's grounding influence. The story concludes with a definitive happy ending, flashing forward to show their beautiful wedding ceremony.

title: The Duke of Death and His Maid
desc: A cursed duke whose touch kills everything searches for a cure so he can finally embrace his relentlessly teasing, deeply loyal maid, culminating in a triumphant and emotional marriage.
wiki: The Duke is exiled by his family to a lonely mansion with only his loyal maid, Alice. Despite his deadly curse, Alice constantly flirts with him, demonstrating her unwavering devotion. Together, they unravel the mystery of the witch Sade who cursed him. After an intense emotional and magical journey, they successfully break the curse. The series ends on an incredibly satisfying and happy note, with the Duke finally able to touch Alice without harming her, leading to their joyous wedding.
img: /static/The Duke of Death and His Maid

title: Dusk Maiden of Amnesia
desc: A boy investigates school mysteries with the ghost of an amnesiac girl, uncovering her tragic past to help her find peace while proving their love can transcend death itself.
wiki: Teiichi Niiya meets the ghost Yuuko Kanoe and forms a paranormal club to solve her murder. He discovers that Yuuko split her soul, casting away all her painful memories into a dark 'Shadow Yuuko'. Teiichi's unconditional love gives Yuuko the strength to accept her dark half and her tragic past. While it seems she will pass on in a heartbreaking final goodbye, the story pulls a heartwarming twist: because she finally let go of her regrets but still deeply desired to stay with Teiichi, she returns as a peaceful spirit, happily remaining by his side.

title: My Love Story with Yamada-kun at Lv999
desc: A heartbroken college student meets a socially awkward pro gamer through an MMO, gradually forming an incredibly healthy, affectionate, and official real-life relationship.
wiki: Akane Kinoshita is dumped by her boyfriend and logs into her MMO to vent, where she meets her aloof guildmate, Akito Yamada. Despite his emotionless exterior in real life, Yamada proves to be incredibly observant and protective. He becomes Akane's comforting presence, helping her move on from her ex. Yamada eventually realizes his own romantic feelings and delivers a remarkably smooth and direct confession. They officially become a couple, and the story continues to showcase their incredibly healthy, sweet, and drama-free dating life.
img: /static/My Love Story with Yamada-kun at Lv999

title: The Ice Guy and His Cool Female Colleague
desc: A descendant of a snow spirit has a massive crush on his kind coworker, creating a cozy office romance that effortlessly transitions from mutual pining to an official relationship.
wiki: Himuro literally freezes his surroundings or summons snowmen when he gets emotional, which happens constantly because he is entirely smitten with his cool-headed coworker, Fuyutsuki. Instead of finding his powers weird, Fuyutsuki provides him with warmth and constant support. The series is a fluffy, stress-free adult romance that avoids heavy drama. They naturally grow closer through office trips and daily interactions, eventually confessing their mutual feelings and establishing a sweet, official romantic relationship with a guaranteed happy trajectory.
img: /static/The Ice Guy and His Cool Female Colleague

title: A Galaxy Next Door
desc: A struggling manga artist accidentally gets engaged to an alien princess, choosing to nullify their magical pact so they can build a genuine, lasting marriage based purely on love.
wiki: Ichiro Kuga hires a new assistant, Shiori Goshiki, who turns out to be an alien princess. Due to a magical accident with her thorn, they form a pact that forces them to share pain and essentially binds them together. However, instead of relying on the forced pact, they actively work to build a genuine relationship. After navigating Shiori's overprotective royal family and successfully nullifying the magical contract, they choose to stay together by their own free will. The story concludes with a wonderfully wholesome happy ending, showing their official wedding.

title: Taisho Otome Fairy Tale
desc: A disabled, exiled heir is sent a cheerful young bride who brings warmth into his bleak existence, resulting in an unbreakable bond that survives a historical disaster.
wiki: Tamahiko Shima is cast away to the mountains by his wealthy, abusive family after an accident leaves him with a disabled right arm. His arranged bride, Yuzuki, arrives and relentlessly brings warmth, optimism, and unconditional love back into his life, completely curing his deep depression. During the devastating 1923 Great Kanto Earthquake, Tamahiko overcomes his trauma and hikes all the way to Tokyo to save Yuzuki. They survive the tragedy, formally cut ties with his toxic family, and achieve a beautifully triumphant happy ending as husband and wife.

title: Tomo-chan is a Girl!
desc: A tomboy tries desperately to get her dense childhood friend to see her as a woman, eventually succeeding in breaking his denial for a deeply satisfying mutual confession.
wiki: Tomo Aizawa confesses her love to Junichiro, but he is so used to treating her as his best 'bro' that he completely misinterprets it. As Tomo tries various comedic ways to show her feminine charm, it is revealed that Junichiro has actually been in love with her for a long time, but was terrified that dating would ruin their friendship. After a period of intense awkwardness and character growth, Junichiro finally proves his resolve by defeating Tomo's intimidating father in a judo match to win his blessing, resulting in them happily becoming an official couple.

title: My Dress-Up Darling
desc: A timid doll-maker helps a popular gyaru create cosplay outfits, sparking a shared passion that rapidly evolves into deep mutual respect and a practically guaranteed romantic future.
wiki: Wakana Gojo, a lonely boy training to be a traditional Hina doll craftsman, is roped into making cosplays for the enthusiastic and beautiful Marin Kitagawa. Rather than mocking his hobby, Marin fiercely defends his talent. Through long nights of sewing, measuring, and attending conventions, Marin develops a massive, undeniable crush on Gojo. While Gojo is slower to realize his own feelings due to low self-esteem, his confidence skyrockets thanks to her support. The series consistently points toward a joyous, happy ending, focusing on their incredibly intimate, highly communicative, and supportive partnership.

title: Hokkaido Gals Are Super Adorable!
desc: A city boy moves to freezing Hokkaido and meets a friendly local gal who helps him adapt, leading to a fiercely loyal romance where he chooses her above all others.
wiki: Tsubasa Shiki moves from Tokyo to the snowy north and meets Minami Fuyuki. Her bold, sweet personality and endless knowledge of Hokkaido help him adjust to his new life. While the story introduces other girls who develop feelings for Tsubasa, he remains entirely devoted to Minami. They overcome misunderstandings, parental expectations, and their own insecurities. The series rewards viewers with a definitive happy ending, as Tsubasa directly rejects the other love interests to officially confess his love to Minami, and they happily start dating.

title: The Girl I Like Forgot Her Glasses
desc: A boy eagerly helps his cute desk neighbor who constantly forgets her glasses, evolving from a one-sided crush into an incredibly wholesome, official middle-school romance.
wiki: Kaede Komura is thrilled when his crush, Ai Mie, constantly forgets or breaks her glasses, as her poor eyesight forces her to get incredibly close to his face and rely on his help throughout the school day. Over time, Mie realizes that she has fallen deeply in love with Kaede, not just because he helps her see, but because of his unwavering kindness. The story builds beautifully to their middle-school graduation, culminating in a heartwarming mutual confession where they officially become a sweet, happy couple.
img: /static/The Girl I Like Forgot Her Glasses

title: Saint Cecilia and Pastor Lawrence
desc: A lazy saint and a dense pastor live peacefully in a small town, sharing a cozy, stress-free dynamic that slowly but surely marches toward a loving, happy future.
wiki: Cecilia is a revered, elegant Saint to the townspeople, but she acts incredibly lazy and spoiled around Pastor Lawrence. She has a massive, obvious crush on him, but Lawrence remains hilariously oblivious to her romantic advances, treating her with pure reverence. Despite his density, Lawrence constantly goes out of his way to protect and pamper her. The series is the epitome of pure fluff, completely devoid of toxic drama or heartbreak, assuring a slow-burn but undeniably happy romantic ending as Lawrence slowly wakes up to the depth of his own feelings for her.

title: Chobits
desc: A student finds an abandoned personal computer girl, ultimately falling in love and proving their bond transcends her programming for a happy future together.
wiki: Hideki Motosuwa discovers Chi, who is eventually revealed to be a legendary 'Chobit' capable of true independent thought and emotion. As Hideki teaches her about the world, he falls in love with her. He learns that if Chi finds her 'person just for her' but is rejected, her core program will wipe the memories of all persocoms on Earth. Despite the societal taboos and the danger she poses, Hideki chooses to love her unconditionally. Chi accepts his love, overriding her destructive reset protocol, and they live happily together as a true, devoted couple.

title: Ah! My Goddess
desc: A college student's wish binds a goddess to him forever, leading to a perfectly wholesome and enduring supernatural romance that eventually results in a formal celestial marriage.
wiki: Keiichi Morisato accidentally calls the Goddess Help Line and wishes for the beautiful Belldandy to stay by his side forever. The wish is granted, and they begin a pure and wholesome life together, facing various comedic and dangerous challenges from demons and heavenly bureaucracy. Their pure love easily overcomes every obstacle. The overarching manga story culminates in a definitive happy ending where Keiichi passes a rigorous divine test, allowing them to be officially and legally married across both the human and divine realms.

title: Waiting in the Summer
desc: A group of friends makes a movie during a complicated summer of love triangles, resulting in a boy and an alien girl falling deeply in love and reuniting against all odds.
wiki: Kaito Kirishima is saved by Ichika, an alien whose ship crashed on Earth. She poses as a transfer student and they spend the summer making a film with their friends, navigating a complex web of unrequited teenage crushes. Kaito and Ichika mutually confess their feelings and share a passionate summer romance. However, Ichika's rescue ship arrives, forcing her to leave Earth and erasing her physical presence. Fortunately, the story delivers a happy ending in the epilogue/OVA, where Ichika successfully returns to Earth to permanently reunite with Kaito.

title: Mayo Chiki!
desc: A boy with a phobia of women discovers a popular butler is actually a girl, leading to a secret-sharing partnership that cures his fears and blossoms into an official romance.
wiki: Kinjiro Sakamachi bleeds from the nose when touched by women due to a severe phobia. He discovers the school's handsome butler, Subaru, is actually a girl in disguise. If her secret is exposed, she will be fired. Kinjiro's gynophobia slowly heals as he spends time fiercely protecting her secret, while Subaru works through her own insecurities about her femininity. In the light novel's climax, Kinjiro completely overcomes his phobia to save her. He successfully confesses his feelings, and the story concludes with them officially dating and living together in a definitive happy ending.

title: My Bride is a Mermaid
desc: A boy forced to marry a mermaid to survive her yakuza family's wrath genuinely falls in love with her, fiercely protecting his new wife in a chaotic but incredibly loyal relationship.
wiki: Nagasumi Michishio is saved from drowning by Sun Seto. To comply with mermaid law and avoid execution by her heavily armed mafia family, he marries her. Nagasumi starts out terrified, but undergoes massive character growth as he constantly risks his life to protect Sun from rival gangs, assassins, and noble merfolk. During the epic final battle, Nagasumi proves his ultimate dedication, proudly declaring his love for his wife and defeating his overpowered rival. They secure a definitive happy ending, happily returning to their chaotic but deeply loving married life.

title: Nodame Cantabile
desc: An elite musician and an eccentric but brilliant pianist push each other to the pinnacle of the classical music world, harmonizing their careers and their romantic lives in Europe.
wiki: Shinichi Chiaki dreams of conducting but is grounded in Japan by a phobia of flying. He meets Megumi 'Nodame' Noda, who is chaotic and messy but plays piano with pure emotion. Nodame helps Chiaki overcome his phobia, and they move to Paris together. Chiaki becomes an acclaimed conductor while Nodame struggles to match his professional pace as a concert pianist. After a period of intense artistic and emotional struggle where they briefly separate, they reconcile. The series features a definitive happy ending with a breathtaking dual performance, cementing their commitment to each other musically and romantically.

title: Love, Chunibyo & Other Delusions!
desc: A boy trying to escape his embarrassing past is drawn into a delusional girl's fantasy world, realizing he loves her exactly as she is and forming a wonderfully sweet, lifelong bond.
wiki: Yuta Togashi wants to leave his embarrassing 'chuunibyou' past behind, but his eccentric classmate Rikka Takanashi forcefully includes him in her fantasy world. Yuta learns that her delusions are a coping mechanism for her father's sudden death. Instead of forcing her to be 'normal,' Yuta embraces his past persona to help her say a proper goodbye to her father. Their relationship is incredibly wholesome and sweet. In the concluding movie, 'Take On Me,' they go on a nationwide road trip to avoid being separated, culminating in a beautiful mutual confession and a kiss, guaranteeing a happy future together.

title: Kodocha
desc: A child actress and a troubled delinquent clash but soon become each other's greatest support systems, navigating heavy emotional trauma to secure a loving relationship as young adults.
wiki: Sana Kurata, a bubbly child actress, takes on the classroom bully, Akito Hayama. They soon uncover each other's deeply troubled family lives and form an unbreakable bond. The story matures significantly as they grow older, dealing with heavy themes like depression, abandonment, and near-fatal injuries. Despite a period of heartbreaking separation and immense psychological struggle in their teenage years, their love for one another never wavers. The manga provides the ultimate happy ending, flashing forward to show them reuniting, getting married, and having a daughter named Sari.

title: Marmalade Boy
desc: A girl navigates an incredibly complicated living situation after her parents swap spouses, ultimately fighting against all odds to secure a happy marriage with her new stepbrother.
wiki: Miki Koishikawa is horrified when her parents divorce, swap partners with another couple, and all move into one massive house. However, she soon falls deeply in love with her new housemate and stepbrother, Yuu Matsuura. They secretly date, facing various love triangles and misunderstandings. Towards the end of the series, they face a massive crisis when they mistakenly believe they are biological half-siblings, forcing a painful breakup. Once they uncover the truth and realize they are not related by blood, they joyfully reconcile. The story happily concludes with them getting married with their eccentric family's full blessing.

title: Engaged to the Unidentified
desc: A girl discovers she is arranged to marry a quiet boy, leading to genuine affection as they uncover shared supernatural childhood secrets and happily choose each other.
wiki: Kobeni Yonomori has to adjust to living with her fiancé Hakuya and his younger sister, Mashiro. She eventually remembers that when they were children, she fell off a cliff and Hakuya saved her. It is revealed that Hakuya is actually a dog-yokai who gave her half of his supernatural power to save her life. Despite the supernatural shock and the initially forced arrangement, Kobeni genuinely falls in love with Hakuya's unwavering devotion. They happily reaffirm their engagement, choosing to be together out of true love rather than obligation.

title: Tsuredure Children
desc: A collection of interconnected short stories where various high schoolers successfully navigate the awkwardness of first love to form incredibly sweet, official relationships.
wiki: Unlike typical romances that drag out a single couple's confession, this series follows multiple different teenagers experiencing the anxiety and joy of confessing their feelings. Characters like the fiercely dense Takano and the patient Sugawara, or the delinquent Ryoko and the deadpan Akagi, work through their hilarious misunderstandings. The series guarantees a multitude of happy endings, as the vast majority of the featured pairs successfully confess, start dating, and share incredibly wholesome, triumphant romantic moments.

title: In/Spectre
desc: The Goddess of Wisdom for spirits and her immortal, future-seeing boyfriend solve supernatural mysteries while maintaining an unshakeable and devoted romantic relationship.
wiki: Kotoko Iwanaga handles supernatural disputes while fiercely loving her boyfriend, Kuro Sakuragawa. Kuro previously consumed the meat of two yokai (a mermaid and a kudagitsu), granting him immortality and the painful ability to alter the future by dying. The story primarily focuses on them battling the urban legend of Steel Lady Nanase, which was created by Kuro's antagonistic cousin, Rikka. Throughout the dark mysteries and Kotoko's constant teasing, Kuro's absolute loyalty and their established, deeply bonded relationship remain a comforting, happy constant.

title: Your Name.
desc: Two teenagers swap bodies across time and space, battling fate to avert a disaster and ultimately reuniting years later for a highly emotional, happy ending.
wiki: Mitsuha and Taki mysteriously swap bodies and fall in love through the notes they leave each other. Taki eventually discovers that Mitsuha's timeline is actually three years in the past, and she died when a comet destroyed her town. Refusing to accept this, Taki drinks her sacred sake to trigger one final swap. They briefly meet during twilight, saving the town, but unfortunately lose their memories of each other's names. However, the film delivers a beautiful happy ending: years later as adults, they pass each other on a staircase in Tokyo, feel an undeniable connection, and tearfully ask for each other's names.

title: Weathering with You
desc: A runaway boy meets a girl who can clear the rainy skies, ultimately choosing to sacrifice the world's weather to save her life and secure their happy future together.
wiki: Hodaka moves to a constantly raining Tokyo and meets Hina, a 'sunshine girl' who can temporarily clear the weather by praying. They start a business bringing sunshine to people, but discover that the more Hina uses her power, the more her body fades away. When she sacrifices herself to the sky to permanently bring back the sun, Hodaka refuses to accept a world without her. He fights his way into the sky to rescue her, declaring he loves her more than clear skies. Tokyo is permanently flooded, but the movie ends happily three years later when Hodaka and Hina joyfully reunite.

title: Josee, the Tiger and the Fish
desc: A college student and a fiercely independent paraplegic artist overcome devastating physical and emotional hurdles to support each other's dreams and secure a happy romance.
wiki: Tsuneo takes a job as a caretaker for Josee, a paraplegic artist who initially treats him harshly. As he helps her experience the outside world, they fall deeply in love. Tragedy strikes when Tsuneo is hit by a car, threatening his ability to walk and his dream to study marine biology in Mexico. Josee illustrates and reads a beautiful storybook to encourage his rehabilitation. They both succeed in their respective recoveries and dreams. The film concludes with a heartwarming happy ending where they confess their love, kiss, and embrace their bright future.

title: Words Bubble Up Like Soda Pop
desc: A boy who speaks through haiku and a self-conscious girl bond over a lost record, culminating in a beautiful, public confession and a sweet, happy romance.
wiki: Cherry wears headphones and expresses his feelings exclusively through haiku to avoid talking, while Smile wears a mask to hide her braces. They spend a vibrant summer together helping an old man find a cherished vinyl record, falling in love along the way. When Cherry's family abruptly moves away, it seems they might miss their chance. In a wonderfully triumphant happy ending, Cherry rushes to the local festival, gets on a stage, and shouts a heartfelt haiku directly to Smile, explicitly praising the buck teeth she is so insecure about. She takes off her mask, smiles widely, and accepts his feelings.

title: Whisper of the Heart
desc: Two ambitious teenagers inspire each other to pursue their artistic dreams, leading to a surprisingly mature commitment and a joyous promise of marriage.
wiki: Shizuku Tsukishima meets Seiji Amasawa, a boy who has been checking out all her library books to get her attention. Seiji is determined to become a master violin maker and travels to Italy to study. Inspired by his immense drive, Shizuku challenges herself to write her first complete novel. They push each other to grow creatively and emotionally. The film ends on an incredibly uplifting and happy note when Seiji returns to Japan early, takes Shizuku to watch the sunrise, and abruptly but sincerely asks her to marry him in the future. She joyfully says yes.

title: Howl's Moving Castle
desc: A young woman cursed to look old and a flawed, vain wizard save each other from war and dark magic, breaking their curses to live happily together.
wiki: Sophie is cursed by the Witch of the Waste to look like an old woman and seeks refuge in the walking castle of the flamboyant wizard, Howl. As Sophie cleans the castle and manages its quirky inhabitants, she and Howl fall deeply in love. Sophie's growing confidence breaks her curse. To save Howl from losing his humanity to his demon pact, Sophie travels into his past and returns his heart to him, successfully breaking his curse as well. The story ends beautifully, showing Howl, Sophie, and their found family flying away to a peaceful, happy life together.

title: Ponyo
desc: A magical goldfish princess gives up her powers to become a human girl and live happily forever with the young boy who loves her unconditionally.
wiki: Ponyo, a magical fish, escapes the ocean and befriends a young boy named Sosuke. She uses powerful magic to become human, but this creates a massive imbalance in nature, plunging the world into a massive flood. To restore the balance and allow Ponyo to stay human permanently, Sosuke must pass a test set by Ponyo's mother, the Goddess of Mercy. He must prove that his love is pure and that he accepts Ponyo whether she is a fish, a mermaid, or a human. He passes without hesitation. They share a sweet kiss, and Ponyo loses her magic to happily become a normal human girl.

title: From Up on Poppy Hill
desc: Two students trying to save their school's clubhouse uncover a shocking shared past, ultimately proving their love and securing a happy future together.
wiki: Umi and Shun work together to preserve the Latin Quarter clubhouse and quickly fall in love. However, their romance halts when they discover a photograph suggesting they might share the same biological father. Heartbroken but determined, they investigate their parents' pasts. They eventually learn that Shun's biological father registered Shun under his own name to protect him before giving him to his current adoptive father, meaning Umi and Shun are not blood-related. Relieved, they confess their love and stay together happily.

title: Emma: A Victorian Romance
desc: A wealthy gentleman and a hardworking maid fight against strict Victorian class divisions, successfully overcoming societal prejudice to secure a happy marriage.
wiki: William Jones, an aristocratic gentleman, falls deeply in love with a quiet maid named Emma. Their relationship faces severe pushback from William's wealthy family and the rigid class structures of Victorian London. Under immense pressure, Emma runs away to the countryside to spare William from scandal. Refusing to give up, William tracks her down, formally abandons his high-society engagement to another woman, and proves his absolute devotion. The series concludes with a beautiful, definitive happy ending as the two finally get married.

title: Amagami SS
desc: A boy experiences different romantic outcomes with six different girls through an omnibus format, guaranteeing a unique, deeply satisfying happy ending for each heroine.
wiki: Junichi Tachibana is afraid of rejection due to a past heartbreak on Christmas Eve. The anime brilliantly uses an omnibus format, resetting the timeline every four episodes. This allows Junichi to successfully romance six different heroines (Haruka, Kaoru, Sae, Ai, Rihoko, and Tsukasa) in completely separate universes. Each girl's arc bypasses harem drama and concludes with a definitive, highly satisfying romantic milestone, such as a flash-forward to their wedding, living together, or an official commitment.

title: Seiren
desc: A high schooler navigates the anxieties of growing up while successfully romancing three different girls in alternate timelines, each culminating in a sweet, definitive conclusion.
wiki: Set in the same universe as Amagami SS, Shoichi Kamita helps three different girls—Hikari, Toru, and Kyouko—with their personal anxieties regarding their futures. Continuing the successful omnibus format of its predecessor, the show resets the timeline for each girl. This structure guarantees that Shoichi achieves a highly satisfying, drama-free happy ending with each heroine in her respective arc, often showing glimpses of their successful futures as couples.

title: Photokano
desc: A boy uses his new passion for photography to connect with the girls at his school, experiencing successful, happy romantic relationships across multiple alternate timelines.
wiki: Kazuya Maeda joins the photography club and begins taking pictures of various girls around his school. Utilizing an omnibus format similar to Amagami SS, the story explores completely separate, parallel routes. In each dedicated timeline, Kazuya uses his photography to help a specific girl overcome her personal insecurities or family issues. Every single route culminates in a mutual confession and a definitively happy romantic ending for that respective couple.

title: Hatsukoi Limited
desc: An interconnected web of middle and high school girls experience the intense trials of first love, successfully navigating crushes to form incredibly sweet, official relationships.
wiki: The series weaves together the romantic lives and first crushes of multiple students, focusing on the awkwardness and emotional intensity of adolescence. Unlike many shows that leave love polygons unresolved, this series actively works to untangle its web of relationships. It provides highly satisfying resolutions by the finale, pairing off the main cast into happy, committed couples as they finally learn to communicate their true, unfiltered feelings to one another.

title: True Tears
desc: A boy caught in a complex love triangle must confront his true feelings, ultimately making a decisive choice that leads to emotional growth and a definitive romantic resolution.
wiki: Shinichiro lives with his long-time childhood crush, Hiromi, who acts cold to hide her true feelings from his strict mother. Concurrently, he bonds with the eccentric Noe, who helps him tap into his artistic passions. After a deeply emotional and dramatic journey where everyone is forced to mature and confront their insecurities, Shinichiro makes a firm decision. He ultimately chooses Hiromi. They reconcile, confess their love, and secure a happy ending together, while Noe moves forward with a renewed spirit.

title: Mashiroiro Symphony: The Color of Lovers
desc: A boy transfers to a prestigious all-girls school, breaking down prejudices and ultimately choosing a loving, happy relationship with a gentle upperclassman.
wiki: Shingo Uryuu joins the Nuko (cat) Club during an integration experiment at an elite all-girls academy. While initially presenting a standard harem dynamic, the anime takes a decisive and refreshing route. Shingo falls deeply in love with the gentle, animal-loving upperclassman, Miu Amaha. They successfully navigate the ensuing jealousy and heartbreak of the other girls, fully committing to each other and securing a highly satisfying, definitive happy ending as an official couple.

title: Hoshizora e Kakaru Hashi
desc: A boy moves to the countryside and accidentally sparks a romance, ultimately choosing the cheerful local girl for a heartwarming and definitive happy ending.
wiki: Kazuma Hoshino moves to a rural town for his brother's health and accidentally kisses a cheerful local girl, Ui Nakatsugawa, on his first day. Though surrounded by multiple romantic interests who develop feelings for him, Kazuma's heart remains set on Ui. In the climax, he definitively rejects the advances of the other girls to confess his true, unwavering love to Ui. She accepts, and the series concludes with them happily walking together as an official, committed couple.

title: Da Capo
desc: A boy navigates a magical island and a complex web of romances, eventually breaking a wish-granting tree's power to secure a genuine, happily-ever-after with his true love.
wiki: Jun'ichi Asakura lives on Hatsune Island, where cherry blossoms bloom year-round due to a magical, wish-granting tree. While multiple girls vie for his affection, the story centers on his deep, evolving bond with his adopted sister, Nemu. The magical tree's wish-granting eventually goes awry, causing Nemu to fall deathly ill. Jun'ichi sacrifices the tree's magic to save her, ending the endless blossoms but securing a beautiful, definitive happy ending where he and Nemu officially marry.

title: Please Teacher!
desc: A boy with a rare condition secretly marries his alien homeroom teacher to protect her identity, turning a marriage of convenience into a profound, lifelong romance.
wiki: Kei Kusanagi suffers from a 'standstill' illness that halts his aging during severe emotional distress. He accidentally discovers his teacher, Mizuho, is an alien. To prevent her from being deported, they get legally married. Despite the secrecy and age gap, they fall genuinely and deeply in love. When Kei's illness relapses and threatens to plunge him into a coma and erase his memories, Mizuho's unwavering devotion pulls him through. The series culminates in a triumphant happy ending where they overcome his illness and remain happily married.

title: ReLIFE
desc: A man relives his final year of high school to fix his life, falling deeply in love and miraculously reuniting with his romantic partner in adulthood for a perfect happy ending.
wiki: 27-year-old Arata Kaizaki takes an experimental pill to look 17 and redo one year of high school to fix his regrets. He falls in love with the socially awkward Chizuru Hishiro. In a massive plot twist, it is revealed that Chizuru is actually another adult ReLIFE test subject. They confess their feelings, but their memories of each other are erased when the experiment ends. However, the story delivers an incredibly satisfying happy ending: they meet as adults at their new workplace, their memories are miraculously triggered, and they happily reunite.

title: Alya Sometimes Hides Her Feelings in Russian
desc: A popular girl flirts with her slacker desk neighbor in Russian, unaware he understands every word, leading to a hilariously sweet dynamic that edges toward genuine romance.
wiki: Alisa (Alya) is the stunning school idol who frequently mutters affectionate remarks in Russian to Masachika Kuze, thinking he is completely clueless. Masachika, secretly fluent in Russian, must hilariously hide his embarrassment. While the light novels are still ongoing, the narrative consistently points toward a highly satisfying romantic payoff, as Masachika becomes her political partner for the student council election and their mutual feelings continue to grow deeper, more supportive, and undeniable.
img: /static/Alya Sometimes Hides Her Feelings in Russian

title: Chillin' in Another World with Level 2 Super Cheat Powers
desc: A discarded merchant gains god-like powers but chooses a peaceful life, marrying a demon wolf and building an incredibly wholesome, drama-free family.
wiki: Flio is summoned as a hero but discarded. Upon reaching level 2, he unlocks infinite magical power but hides it to avoid the human-demon war. He defeats and subsequently marries Fenrys, a powerful demon wolf. The series completely bypasses typical harem tropes, focusing entirely on Flio and Fenrys's fiercely loyal and highly affectionate married life. They establish a peaceful household, adopt magical companions, and continually guarantee a stress-free, happy ending in every story arc.
img: /static/Chillin' in Another World with Level 2 Super Cheat Powers

title: An Archdemon's Dilemma: How to Love Your Elf Bride
desc: A socially awkward sorcerer rescues a beautiful elf, transforming his dark life into an incredibly sweet, domestic romance where they mutually heal and officially become a loving couple.
wiki: Zagan, an evil sorcerer terrible at communication, buys the elf slave Nephy at an underground auction solely because he fell in love at first sight. He frees her immediately, and they begin a wholesome cohabitation. Zagan uses his immense power primarily to protect her and provide a comfortable life. They slowly overcome their severe awkwardness and past traumas, explicitly confess their feelings, and share a definitive happy ending as a deeply devoted, official couple.
img: /static/An Archdemon's Dilemma: How to Love Your Elf Bride

title: Endo and Kobayashi Live! The Latest on Tsundere Villainess Lieselotte
desc: Two high schoolers act as gods to guide a dating sim prince, successfully saving the misunderstood villainess from a tragic fate and securing multiple joyous marriages.
wiki: Aoto Endo and Shihono Kobayashi provide live commentary on an otome game, which Prince Siegwald actually hears as divine guidance. By explaining that his seemingly wicked fiancée Lieselotte is just a tsundere (hiding her massive love for him with harsh words), they completely change the game's tragic narrative. Siegwald aggressively romances Lieselotte, saving her from possession by the Witch of Yore. The story ends incredibly happily, with Siegwald and Lieselotte marrying, and Endo and Kobayashi also confessing their love in the real world.
img: /static/Endo and Kobayashi Live! The Latest on Tsundere Villainess Lieselotte
